articles.headline: Houthis claim joint missile attack on Israel with Iran and Hezbollah
articles.excerpt: Yemen’s Houthis say they launched a missile attack on Israel in coordination with Iran and Hezbollah.

articles.headline: ‘An incredible feeling’: Turkiye beat Kosovo to end 24-year World Cup wait
articles.excerpt: Turkiye secure a return to football's biggest tournament for the first time since 2002, when they finished third.

articles.headline: US messages via Witkoff and intermediaries are not negotiations: Araghchi
articles.excerpt: Iran's foreign minister says message exchanges continue with Washington, but insists there are no negotiations or trust.

articles.headline: India begins world’s largest population census
articles.excerpt: More than three million Indian officials have begun the yearlong task of counting the country’s population.

articles.headline: ‘Gerrymandering’ in India’s Assam cuts Muslim representation before vote
articles.excerpt: Muslims formed the majority in about 35 of the state’s 126 constituencies. Now that is down to about 20 seats.

articles.headline: War on Iran: Three key takeaways from Araghchi’s interview with Al Jazeera
articles.excerpt: Araghchi confirmed direct talks with Witkoff but denied that they constituted negotiations in an exclusive interview.

articles.headline: Israeli police inspect damage on the road after missile impact
articles.excerpt: Police inspect a strike site in Tel Aviv after a missile hit the city.

articles.headline: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE
articles.excerpt: Shrapnel from an Iranian drone interception kills a Bangladeshi national in the UAE's Fujairah city.

articles.headline: What is NASA’s Artemis II moon mission, and when will it launch?
articles.excerpt: For the first time in more than 50 years, humans will travel around the moon to test deep-space life-support systems.

articles.headline: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war…
articles.excerpt: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

articles.headline: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
articles.excerpt: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

articles.headline: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
articles.excerpt: The rapper "started feeling very ill" mid-way through the show in New York, her representative told US media.

articles.headline: More than 70 miners killed in South Sudan as government and opposition…
articles.excerpt: Both government and opposition groups have been accused of being responsible for the deadly attack.

articles.headline: Kissing a woman's hand can be sexual assault, Spanish court finds
articles.excerpt: The Supreme Court found that a man had done more than harass a woman by kissing her hand as there had been "a clear sexual component" to his behaviour.

articles.headline: Céline Dion announces comeback shows: 'I'm so ready for this'
articles.excerpt: The star was diagnosed with a rare condition that affects her singing voice and her ability to walk.

articles.headline: Most Syrian refugees in Germany expected to return home in three years…
articles.excerpt: Merz’s government has taken a tougher line on refugees and migrants, amid surging support for the anti-immigration AfD party.

articles.headline: Hegseth calls on US allies to 'step up' over Strait of Hormuz
articles.excerpt: The defence secretary's comments came after US President Donald Trump told other nations to "go get your own oil".

articles.headline: Woods to 'step away and seek treatment' after crash
articles.excerpt: Tiger Woods says he is "stepping away for a period of time to seek treatment and focus on my health" following an arrest after a car crash.

articles.headline: US Supreme Court strikes down Colorado's ban on conversion therapy
articles.excerpt: It sided 8-1 with a therapist from Colorado Springs who argued the ban violated her free speech rights.

articles.headline: How will the Artemis astronauts go to the toilet in space?
articles.excerpt: For the ten day mission, Nasa scientists have developed a new 'universal waste management system', which is basically a space loo.

articles.headline: Estate agents accuse Rightmove of charging excessive fees
articles.excerpt: The online listing portal is now being pursued in a class action, launched on behalf of potentially hundreds of estate agents.

articles.headline: Minimum wage rises to £12.71 an hour as firms warn of impact
articles.excerpt: Many businesses have said they will have to pass higher wage costs onto customers.

articles.headline: From water to council tax: How the bill rises (and one drop) affect you
articles.excerpt: A string of bill increases have taken effect but minimum wage and benefit rises will help some to pay them.

articles.headline: Oil briefly falls below $100 and shares jump on Trump Iran war pledge
articles.excerpt: European stock markets opened higher after the US president said the conflict would "end very soon".

articles.headline: Topps Tiles to close 23 stores over rising costs
articles.excerpt: Topps Tiles says eight stores have already closed - with the rest to shut over the next six months.

articles.headline: Oil nears highest price since start of Iran war
articles.excerpt: The US-Israel Iran war has halted almost all traffic in a key waterway and the price Brent crude has surged.

articles.headline: Bitcoin ETFs post $1.3B in March inflows, first monthly gain of 2026
articles.excerpt: US spot Bitcoin ETFs ended Q1 in the red, with about $500 million of net outflows despite March inflows, as sentiment remained weak amid geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: New Hampshire’s Bitcoin-backed bond gets Moody's rating
articles.excerpt: Moody's assigned a provisional Ba2 rating to New Hampshire’s bond project, placing it below investment grade, as it cited Bitcoin’s volatility.

articles.headline: Bitcoin’s $10K range expected to hold until spot traders show up: Data
articles.excerpt: Futures-driven trading and weak spot demand have kept Bitcoin range-bound, with limited buy-side interest capping the duration of price breakouts.

articles.headline: Ethereum bulls must hold $2K: Volatility metric hints at ‘strong’ move next
articles.excerpt: A sharp drop in Ether’s realized volatility could result in significant ETH price moves if history repeats, making $2,000 a key support level to keep an eye on.

articles.headline: XRP price charts flash bottom signals as bulls defend $1.30
articles.excerpt: Technical indicators hinted at a possible reversal in XRP’s price, as traders watch whether key support levels can hold.

articles.headline: CFTC’s top enforcer puts prediction market insider traders on notice
articles.excerpt: “There’s a myth in mainstream media and social media that insider trading doesn’t apply in the prediction markets … That is wrong,” said CFTC enforcement director David Miller.

articles.headline: Massachusetts city to weigh crypto ATM ban, citing financial risks
articles.excerpt: Haverhill City Council, following other jurisdictions, is set to consider an ordinance banning crypto ATMs viewed as enabling financial fraud and money laundering.

articles.headline: Bitcoin’s old peaks aren’t untouchable anymore, and the days of parabolic…
articles.excerpt: Bitcoin’s price retraces to old highs, signaling slower growth and a maturing market.

articles.headline: Crypto asset manager CoinShares to list on Nasdaq after $1.2 billion SPAC…
articles.excerpt: The listing makes CoinShares the latest crypto firm to go public and follows similar moves by BitGo, Circle, Bullish, and Gemini in recent years.

articles.headline: Strategy's STRC keeps dividend payout steady at 11.5% after seven straight…
articles.excerpt: The perpetual preferred yield holds at 11.5% for April as the 30-day volume weighted average price stabilizes near $100.

articles.headline: These catalysts could bump bitcoin as Trump hands three-week target to end…
articles.excerpt: Asian stocks surged 4% and S&P 500 futures jumped after Trump said the conflict could conclude without a deal with Tehran, while Morgan Stanley's newly approved bitcoin ETF at 14 basis points opens a $6.2 trillion…

articles.headline: XRP holds $1.34 as supply tightens but price fails to break higher
articles.excerpt: Record outflows and rising scarcity suggest accumulation, yet failure to break higher keeps setup unresolved.

articles.headline: Bitcoin enters the public bond market as Moody’s gives a first-of-its-kind…
articles.excerpt: A New Hampshire state authority is set to issue a first-of-its-kind bitcoin-backed bond with a Ba2 rating, marking an early test of how crypto can function as collateral inside traditional public finance markets.

articles.headline: Crypto Startup Uses Polymarket to Bet on Its Own Fundraise, Blindsiding…
articles.excerpt: P2P.me was established to push boundaries, but the startup admitted that wagering on itself via Polymarket may have been a bridge too far.

articles.headline: If You Hold Solana on Magic Eden's Wallet, It's Time to Move It or Lose It
articles.excerpt: Support for Magic Eden's crypto wallet is ending soon, meaning users will have to export their private keys to avoid losing access to funds.

articles.headline: Bitcoin, Crypto Stocks Climb on Reports That Iran's President Is 'Ready to…
articles.excerpt: Stocks are surging while Bitcoin and Ethereum hit their highest prices in days after Iran's president said he's looking to end the conflict.

articles.headline: Tether's USAT Stablecoin Expands Beyond Ethereum Mainnet to Celo
articles.excerpt: The Tether-backed USAT stablecoin built for the U.S. market is expanding to Ethereum layer-2 network Celo with help from Google Cloud.

articles.headline: Stablecoin Market to Hit $2 Trillion in 2028 Even as Velocity Doubles…
articles.excerpt: Standard Chartered says stablecoin velocity has doubled in two years, driven by USDC's new use cases in TradFi and AI payments.

articles.headline: Iran threatens Nvidia, Apple and other tech giants with attack
articles.excerpt: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has warned a number of tech companies with Middle East operations they'll be considered "legitimate targets."

articles.headline: Oracle cutting thousands in latest layoff round as company continues to…
articles.excerpt: Oracle has ratcheted up its capital expenditures as it builds data center infrastructure that can handle AI workloads.

articles.headline: Trump to address nation on Iran war Wednesday night, White House says
articles.excerpt: President Donald Trump said he expected that U.S. military forces will leave Iran in "two or three weeks."

articles.headline: Oil prices seesaw around $100 in volatile trading after Trump war exit…
articles.excerpt: Crude prices dropped on Wednesday as President Donald Trump signaled willingness to exit the Iran conflict even as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

articles.headline: Silver just had its worst month in 15 years. Where investors see it going…
articles.excerpt: Silver shed almost 20% in March, marking its worst monthly performance since September 2011.

articles.headline: Trump signs executive order limiting mail-in voting ahead of 2026 U.S…
articles.excerpt: Trump has put pressure on Congress to pass the SAVE America Act, a measure that would require photo identification and proof of U.S. citizenship to vote.

articles.headline: NFL approves sale of 7% of Las Vegas Raiders at over $11 billion valuation
articles.excerpt: The National Football League has approved the sale of 7% of the Las Vegas Raiders at a valuation of more than $11 billion.

articles.headline: Crypto asset manager CoinShares to begin trading on Nasdaq through SPAC…
articles.excerpt: CoinShares is merging with Vine Hill Capital to form the holding company CoinShares PLC in a deal valuing the business at about $1.2 billion.

articles.headline: Jim Cramer: Three ways the stock market will flip if the U.S.-Iran war ends
articles.excerpt: Want to know how the market will react when the war's over? CNBC's Jim Cramer says Tuesday's session gives investors a hint.

articles.headline: Tiger Woods says he is seeking treatment after DUI rollover crash in…
articles.excerpt: President Donald Trump, whose former daughter-in-law Vanessa Trump is dating Woods, told The New York Post that Woods "lives a life of pain."

articles.headline: News24 | Good News Day Top 10 | A mom’s salt empire, a coach’s Bafana…
articles.excerpt: It’s Good News Day on News24 and we have 10 brilliant stories to give you this April Feels Day. From a local success story of a SA mom who created the Oryx salt brand, to a soccer coach who raised money to take 50 Cape…

articles.headline: News24 | Cape Town runner’s ‘endless loop’ challenge helps kids get free…
articles.excerpt: A Cape Town runner has taken on one of the toughest endurance challenges on the calendar – not just to test his limits, but to raise funds for children in need of life-changing surgery.

articles.headline: News24 | Coca-Cola investing almost R20bn in SA over 4 years
articles.excerpt: Coca-Cola and its two authorised bottling partners in South Africa plan to invest R17.6 billion in domestic operations through 2030 to expand capacity and bolster distribution.

articles.headline: News24 | Ubuntu in the UAE: Durbanite in Dubai opens doors to stranded…
articles.excerpt: At just 24 years old, Makida Kumalo left South Africa for the UAE’s Airbnb industry, but she carried with her the spirit of Ubuntu, opening her doors to stranded fellow South Africans during the Dubai bombings.

articles.headline: News24 | WATCH | Meet the six-year-old author who's turned storytelling…
articles.excerpt: Six-year-old Tlotlo Tsheka, a Grade 2 pupil at SPARK Witpoortjie, is turning imagination into impact, publishing three books and launching a literacy mission to inspire other children to read, write, and dream bigger.

articles.headline: News24 | ‘We’ll be leaving very soon’: Trump hints at winding down Iran…
articles.excerpt: US President Donald Trump said the United States could end its military attacks on Iran within two to three weeks and Tehran did not have to make a deal as a prerequisite for the conflict to wind down.
